text: About Us - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) About Us About PNEC Pakistan Navy Engineering College was initially affiliated with Karachi University. In 1977 the affiliation of PNEC was transferred to NED University of Engineering & Technology. The college was shifted to its present location in 1982 and commissioned as PNS JAUHAR. In 1995 it became the constituent College of National University Of Sciences and Technology (NUST) Islamabad. The college conducts undergraduate courses in disciplines of Electrical, Mechanical and Naval Architecture. The College also conducts MS and PhD programs in Mechanical, Electrical and Manufacturing Engineering for service and civilian students. PNEC also stands out by launching MS in the specialised field of Naval Architecture and Cyber Security from Fall 18 to Spring 20 respectively. COMMANDANT MESSAGE Engr Mudassar Khurshid PNEC from its initial beginnings as an engineering school that was established back in 1962 and affiliated with NED University Karachi, has transformed and transitioned into a large constituent campus of National University of Sciences and Technology (NUST); serving as an iconic centre of excellence in engineering education. Extending the frontiers of education, PNEC started MS programme in 80s and PhD programme in 2000. It was the first NUST constituent college from where PhD scholar graduated under NUST. Moving with the time, PNEC continues to offer and add more disciplines to its fold. What distinguishes PNEC from others is our research throughout which is more focused towards application, providing solutions to technological challenges facing the organizations. Research projects from Pakistan Navy, ICT R&D, HEC, Maritime Security Agency, to name a few. I stand convinced that has all been realizable because of our dedicated and qualified faculty who have been inspiring and mentoring students. Our graduates and their successful careers are further evidences to the quality of training and mentoring they have received being a student at this university. With global footprint, our alumni extends to the stretches of the world. text: Departments - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) Departments PNEC Departments Department of Engineering Sciences The department of engineering sciences has its prime objective of producing highly skilled mechanical engineers with specialization in three areas namely, Thermal Fluids, Thermal Powers and Computational Statistics. The department is equipped with modern laboratories, research facilities and state of art computer centers. Focused emphasis on practical exposure is also given to mechanical engineers along with theoretical instructional, with the help of qualified faculty and several well equipped laboratories. Department of Electronics & Power Engineering The Electrical Engineering (EE) curriculum encompasses a broad background in the analysis and synthesis of electrical and electronic circuits and devices, in the mathematical tools necessary for signal processing, and in the design and analysis of modern electrical systems. At PNEC, students obtain an extensive insight into electrical engineering through courses and laboratory experience that include understanding contemporary issues that have global and societal impact. Department of Cyber Security Cyber Security Department has been established at NUST- PNEC in June 2019. The primary objective of the department in to effective run and manage the MS Cyber Security Program which has been launched w.e.f Spring 2020 after obtaining NOC from HEC in Sep 2019. Our aim is to make it centre of Academic Excellence in Cyber Defense Education and Research in Pakistan and the World. Department of Naval Architecture Department of Naval Architecture is a recently established department and was formed in the year 2018. Both Postgraduate & Undergraduate programs in the field of Naval Architecture are being offered. A naval architect is a professional engineer who is responsible for the design, classification, survey, construction, and repair of both commercial and military ships including yachts, powerboats, fishing boats, barges, warships, cruise ships, ice breakers, submersibles, submarines and offshore structures. Department of Maritime Sciences This department facilitates PN Operations Branch Officers to optimally exploit the modern weapons and sensors. For this purpose, BS (MS) degree program through hybrid distance learning has been introduced in the training scheme. The faculty is a blend of Management and Information Technology professionals. Department of Applied Sciences Welcome to the Faculty of Applied Sciences at Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC). We are committed to teaching and research excellence in Applied Sciences. We have a faculty well equipped with pedagogy and teaching learning techniques. The department undertakes intellectual grooming of BE and MS students in pure sciences (Physics, Chemistry

text: (MS) degree program through hybrid distance learning has been introduced in the training scheme. The faculty is a blend of Management and Information Technology professionals. Department of Applied Sciences Welcome to the Faculty of Applied Sciences at Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC). We are committed to teaching and research excellence in Applied Sciences. We have a faculty well equipped with pedagogy and teaching learning techniques. The department undertakes intellectual grooming of BE and MS students in pure sciences (Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics) and humanities (English, Pakistan Study and Islamiat). The department is aligned with PNEC s academic Mission to provide high-quality, student-centered learning experiences. Department of Industrial and Manufacturing Engineering The department of Manufacturing Engineering and Management offers post-graduate degree courses in manufacturing engineering and management. The program is designed to cater for the challenging requirement of the manufacturing industry while offering courses in technical management to equip the graduates with both technical and management skills. Department of Computer Science Computer Science Department has been established at NUST- PNEC in June 2025. The primary objective of the department in to effective run and manage the BSCS and MS Data Science Programs which has been launched w.e.f Fall 2025 after obtaining NOC from NCEAC & HEC in July 2025.

text: WELCOME TO PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) About Us WELCOME TO PNEC WELCOME TO PNEC Committed to E xcellence!! Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) welcomes you to the only constituent college of National University of Sciences & Technology (NUST) at Karachi with a progressive vision for your future academic goals. PNEC has established a professional reputation for excellence in teaching and research because of its high academic standards, highly qualified faculty, and state-of-the-art research/ lab facilities available to students and faculty. The primary role of PN Engineering College is to produce Quality Engineers for Pakistan Navy & NUST. To achieve this, it conducts degree courses in the disciplines of Engineering, Maritime Sciences, Naval Architecture, AI & Cyber Security. These courses are also availed by other Defense Services and friendly countries. Apart from degree courses, the college also provides specialized mid-career courses for PN technical officers. These include Marine Engineering Systems Management Course (MESMC) & Weapons Engineering Systems Management Course (WESMC), specially designed to meet the service requirements of PN officers in their career.
title: WELCOME TO PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: COMMANDANT MESSAGE - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) About Us COMMANDANT MESSAGE COMMANDANT MESSAGE PNEC from its humble beginnings as an engineering school that was established back in 1962 and affiliated with NED University Karachi, has transformed and transitioned into a large constituent campus of National University of Sciences and Technology (NUST); serving as an iconic centre of excellence in engineering education. Extending the frontiers of education, PNEC started MS programme in 80s and PhD programme in 1998.It was the first NUST constituent college from where PhD scholar graduated under NUST. Moving with the time, PNEC continues to offer and add more disciplines to its fold. What distinguishes PNEC from others is our research throughput, which is more focused towards application, providing solutions to technological challenges facing the organizations. Research projects from Pakistan Navy, ICT R&D, HEC, Maritime Security Agency, to name a few. I stand convinced that it has all been realizable because of our dedicated and qualified faculty who have been inspiring and mentoring students. Our graduates and their successful careers are further evidences to the quality of training and mentoring they have received being a student at this university. With global footprint, our alumni extend to the stretches of the world. Cognizant of PNEC legacy that is a source of great contentment, I am fully mindful of the vision that a leader in my capacity must possess in order to enable the students, faculty and staff to pursue an even more ambitious and innovative agenda. With a view to enhance the pedestal of this College, I have chalked out my areas of priority focus: restructuring PNEC through better all-inclusive infrastructure, value-addition in our engineering programmes and postgraduate studies, augmenting solution-oriented research, revitalizing the international academic and industry linkages, strengthening the financial status, and greater participation of our students in societal and sports event of repute. Through my concerted efforts towards these aspects and with the continuing support of my faculty and staff, we shall continue creating an environment for our students that inspires them to discover their engineering potential in the most congenial environments. We will continue to expand the dimensions of learning, discovery, innovation and change by desiring unique perspectives, enriching experiences and strategic initiatives. Every college or university has a culture that develops with time as the institution ages and matures. In all diversity, however, there is one common ground, to aim for academic excellence.
title: COMMANDANT MESSAGE - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

We will continue to expand the dimensions of learning, discovery, innovation and change by desiring unique perspectives, enriching experiences and strategic initiatives. Every college or university has a culture that develops with time as the institution ages and matures. In all diversity, however, there is one common ground, to aim for academic excellence. With this as a driving force, and with the availability of resources, any institution can achieve the goal of providing the best education, and producing the best engineers, scientists, scholars and above all knowledge-based citizens of Pakistan and the planet we live in. Engr Mudassar Khurshid Commandant, Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)
title: COMMANDANT MESSAGE - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: DEAN MESSAGE - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) About Us DEAN MESSAGE DEAN MESSAGE Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) remains persistent in its commitment to innovation and embracing challenges as opportunities for growth and learning. At PNEC, we offer a range of value-added courses designed in alignment with NUST, HEC, PEC and NCEAC policies, aimed at instilling in you the drive to push beyond the limits set by self or other influencers. The opportunities at PNEC are limitless not only will you acquire knowledge, but you will also refine your personality, emerging as a confident individual and a skilled professional engineer. PNEC has a proud legacy of producing exceptional engineers in fields such as Mechanical, Electrical, Industrial & Manufacturing, Cyber Security, and Naval Architecture. The feedback we receive from our alumni is highly gratifying, as it reflects the success of our graduates who are contributing significantly to defense and various prestigious industries both within Pakistan and internationally. Additionally, our service students from the Pakistan Navy, enrolled in the BS (MS) degree program conducted through hybrid distance learning, are excelling in their duties on board PN ships. PNEC is an institution dedicated to fulfilling students aspirations for excellence in technical education. Our motto, Commitment to Excellence, embodies our unwavering dedication to providing top-quality education that meets the ever-evolving demands of today s industry. We achieve this through continuous refinement and updating of our course curricula. In addition to technical expertise, I firmly believe that moral values are equally essential. These values must be instilled in our future engineers and technocrats, ensuring they become not only highly skilled professionals but also honest and responsible citizens. PNEC emphasis on the moral training of students. With these principles deeply ingrained and actively practiced, PNEC is one of the best institutes in the region and is counted as an institute of national repute, Alhamdullilah. Engr Zulfiqar Ahmed Khan Dean, Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)
title: DEAN MESSAGE - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: Professor Krzysztof Marasek's Best Paper Award - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) In The Spotlights Professor Krzysztof Marasek s Best Paper Award We are honoured to share that our research paper, A Review of Recent Advancements in Immersive Telepresence, has been awarded with the Professor Krzysztof Marasek Best Paper Award in Digital Interaction at the 12th MIDI Conference, held on December 12, 2024, in Warsaw Poland. This distinction, accompanied by a 500 EUR special prize, recognizes our contributions to the advancement of immersive telepresence technologies and underscores the university s commitment to advancing research in digital interaction and emerging technologies.
title: Professor Krzysztof Marasek's Best Paper Award - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: Congratulations to the Brightest Stars of NUST PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) In The Spotlights Congratulations to the Brightest Stars of NUST PNEC Dawlance is proud to announce that two outstanding students from NUST PNEC have been awarded the prestigious Golden Keys among 26 interns selected from top universities of Pakistan, Golden Key a symbolic representation of their status as the top interns who have earned the opportunity to join Dawlance. Not only this, each of these talented individuals will also be awarded Dawlance appliances worth PKR 200,000.
title: Congratulations to the Brightest Stars of NUST PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: NUST-PNEC Formula Student Team's achievement at Formula Student UK 2023 - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) In The Spotlights NUST-PNEC Formula Student Team s achievement at Formula Student UK 2023 NUST-PNEC Formula Student Team has once again demonstrated its engineering prowess and global competitiveness by participating in the prestigious Formula Student UK 2023, Concept Class on 19 to 23 July 2023. This renowned competition attracted top-notch teams from around the world and is considered the largest engineering competition on the global stage. Remarkably, NFST achieved a historic feat for Pakistan by becoming the first and only team from the country to secure a category victory in the LTS (Lap Time Simulation) Event. The team showcased their innovative and well-designed vehicle, earning them a commendable 6th position in the Design Event, while also excelling in the Cost and Manufacturing Event, securing another 6th position. With their exceptional performance in all aspects of the competition, NUST-PNEC Formula Student Team clinched an outstanding overall 5th position in the Concept Class category, a remarkable achievement that solidifies their standing as one of the top teams in the international Formula Student circuit. This accomplishment reflects the team s dedication, expertise, and determination in the field of automotive engineering.
title: NUST-PNEC Formula Student Team's achievement at Formula Student UK 2023 - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: NUST-PNEC 37th Convocation Ceremony - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News NUST-PNEC 37th Convocation Ceremony NUST-PNEC 37th Convocation Ceremony December 19, 2025 - December 19, 2025 37th Convocation of NUST-PNEC was held on 19th December 25 at Bahria Auditorium Karachi. Engineer Syed Murad Ali Shah CHIEF MINISTER Sindh was the chief guest for the occasion. V/Admiral Muhammad Faisal Abbasi Hilal e Imtiaz Military, Commander Karachi, also graced the event with his presence. A total of 353 graduates were awarded degrees, the same included 4 PhD, 41 MS students and 308 undergraduate students. The chief guest also awarded 39 medals to outstanding position holders which included 7 president Gold medals, 4 CNS Gold medals, 8 Silver medals, 18 Rector Gold medals and 2 Quaid-e-Azam Gold medals.
title: NUST-PNEC 37th Convocation Ceremony - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: RECTOR NUST DR MUHAMMAD ZAHID LATIF VISITED PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News RECTOR NUST DR MUHAMMAD ZAHID LATIF VISITED PNEC RECTOR NUST DR MUHAMMAD ZAHID LATIF VISITED PNEC December 21, 2024 - December 21, 2024 Rector NUST, Dr Muhammad Zahid Latif visited NUST-PNEC on 21 Dec 24. Upon arrival he was received by Commandant NUST-PNEC Engr Mudassar Khurshid. Rector was introduced to Dean and all HoDs. Rector was given college presentation in conference room. Pro Rector (Acad) Dr Osama Hasan and Registrar NUST Engr Muhammad Ashfaq Maitla also joined the presentation. At the end of presentation, Rector took the round of College area which was followed by interaction with College Faculty Members at College Tea Bar.
title: RECTOR NUST DR MUHAMMAD ZAHID LATIF VISITED PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: NUST-PNEC OPEN HOUSE 2025 - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News NUST-PNEC OPEN HOUSE 2025 NUST-PNEC OPEN HOUSE 2025 May 24, 2025 - May 24, 2025 The NUST PNEC Open House and Career Fair 2025 was successfully held on 24th May 2025 at the Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC), a constituent college of NUST. The event was graced by Mr. Ahmed Azeem Alvi , President of the SITE Association of Industry, as the Chief Guest . The event witnessed enthusiastic participation from over 150 industry representatives from 75 renowned companies , including 16 Chief Executive Officers (CEOs) . Final-year students showcased more than 30 innovative projects , reflecting their engineering prowess, creativity, and commitment to solving real-world challenges. Visiting professionals showed keen interest in the student projects and commended the quality and relevance of the work presented. Several companies conducted preliminary job interviews on-site , providing students with valuable employment opportunities ahead of graduation. In addition to student-industry interaction, meaningful discussions were held between faculty members and industry leaders on potential avenues for collaborative research and development , marking a step forward in strengthening industry-academia linkages.
title: NUST-PNEC OPEN HOUSE 2025 - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

CEO OTF Mr Omer Mateen Allawala along with partner donor from The Soorty Foundation taken the interviews of NUST-PNEC students.
title: Shaping Futures: Opportunity Knocks at PNEC with OTF - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: NUST PNEC Students Shine at DUHS-DICE Health Innovation Exhibition! - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News NUST PNEC Students Shine at DUHS-DICE Health Innovation Exhibition! NUST PNEC Students Shine at DUHS-DICE Health Innovation Exhibition! February 25, 2025 - February 25, 2025 On 25th Feb 2025, A team of 10 NUST-PNEC Students presented four innovative projects at the 8th DUHS-DICE Health Innovation Exhibition. Their hard work, supported by the Power System Research Lab (PRL) at NUST-PNEC, was highly appreciated by judges and visitors alike. A major highlight was their Final Year Project (FYP), titled Smart Ambulance Response System, which won the Special Innovation Prize and a cash award of Rs 10,000/-. This project has the potential to significantly improve emergency medical response and save lives. This incredible journey showcases the talent and dedication of students and the excellent support provided by the Power System Research Lab.
title: NUST PNEC Students Shine at DUHS-DICE Health Innovation Exhibition! - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: K-Electric Visited NUST-PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News K-Electric Visited NUST-PNEC K-Electric Visited NUST-PNEC February 26, 2025 - February 26, 2025 On 26th February 2025, K-Electric Pvt Ltd conducted a Recruitment Drive at NUST PNEC, providing students with an excellent opportunity to join their Emerging Talent Programme. The event commenced with an insightful session by K-Electric representatives, who guided students on job opportunities, resume building, and effective interviewing techniques. Furthermore, the session highlighted the company s work culture, professional growth prospects, and career pathways within K-Electric. The initiative was highly appreciated, strengthening industry-academia collaboration and creating a pathway for aspiring young engineers to connect with Pakistan s leading power company.
title: K-Electric Visited NUST-PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: STUDENT SOCIETIES & CLUBS - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) About Us STUDENT SOCIETIES & CLUBS STUDENT SOCIETIES & CLUBS PNEC, being a constituent college of NUST, undertakes many activities in addition to the class room instructions and labs. One such category of activities are those undertaken by various student societies, which are an essential part of any university campus. The main aim of student societies is to develop communication, organization and leadership skills. Societies promote team work and social ethics, development of new skills and abilities, exploration of new talents, and aid to the core objectives of a degree programme. Currently, 12 societies are registered in PNEC that fall under various categories such as social service, alumni, debating, industrial relation, academic, sports etc. The list of student societies registered in PNEC is as under: NO NAME OF SOCIETIES PURPOSE AIR Society NUST PNEC Alumni and Industrial Relations Society To create a bond and linkage between the existing and graduated students of PNEC-NUST. In addition to this, aim of societies is execution of Industrial Projects and placement of Pakistan Navy Engineering College students in Industries 2. ASHRAE NUST PNEC Student Chapter American Society for Heat Refrigeration and Air Conditioning Engineers The objectives of ASHRAE Society is the advancement of the sciences of heating, refrigerating and air-conditioning engineering and related sciences; the continuing education of the members and other interested persons in said sciences through lectures, demonstrations and publications; the rendition of career guidance to students of sciences and the encouragement of the scientific research. 3. ECSS NUST PNEC Education and Community Services Society ECSS is a social service society which was established for the site purpose of aiding and contributing to the underprivileged segment of the society. It tends to perpetuate its philanthropic cause for the betterment of society. It has actively carried out various activities inside and outside the university premises in order to raise funds and donation which are invested in charity. 4. The society promotes wholesome interaction of the students with the pioneers and renowned leaders of the field

The SME provides national and international level opportunities for the students of PNEC to start their professional career and create a link between the industrial experts, industrial entrepreneurs and the manufacturing students. The activities of SME are not only based on or related to academics but also involve recreational and technical aspects in an attempt to groom students to rise as professional individuals. 7. WIE NUST PNEC Student Society Women In Engineering Women in Engineering Society aims are to break down gender stereotypes within engineering by providing opportunities for young girls and women already at the university. As many people know, there is a lack of women deciding to study or choose a career in engineering. The society aims to break down stereotypes and show what engineering is and that it is an interesting and rewarding choice. 9. MACS Media Affairs and Communications Society The Media Affairs and Communications Society (commonly known as MACS) is a student body at Pakistan Navy Engineering College whose primary aim is the promotion of NUST s constituent college in Karachi. The society works towards this objective in a number of ways, which include but are not limited to; organizing and managing recreational events at the college, sending teams to media and MUN competitions outside the college, and covering the college s progress and its events in online and printed publications. Being one of the few non-technical societies at PNEC, MACS covers a wide range of departments. Besides tending to media affairs and giving a platform to musical and dramatic enthusiasts at the college, MACS arranges events and activities that cater to the entertainment of the students and faculty at PNEC, as a whole. It also works in collaboration with other societies at PNEC to promote their events, and also cover them in its publications. MACS, therefore, is widely acclaimed as the most active society at PNEC. MACS also give the engineers-in-making, a chance to polish their non-technical skills, which are likely to help them with their practical life in the future. The students are given opportunities through continuous training to groom their presentation, oratory, time-management, planning, coordination, record-keeping and miscellaneous other skills which are vital for a successful professional career. 10. STUDENT COUNCILS PNEC students are divided into small groups of approximately 35 students each. Each group is supervised by a Faculty Advisor (faculty member). These officers liaise with each other to guide and counsel students on academic as well as personal matters. They also update parents about their children s performance regularly. In addition, a continuous liaison with the Center for Counseling and Career Advisory (C3A) at central campus is maintained for guidance. SPORTS AT PNEC NUST PNEC offers a wide variety of sports activities. All College. Regular inter-college/school sports competitions are held every year at the University. PNEC teams have been participating and performing well in HEC inter-varsity sports competitions both at the zonal and national levels. text: CHRONOLOGICAL HISTORY - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) About Us CHRONOLOGICAL HISTORY CHRONOLOGICAL HISTORY History at a Glance Since independence, none of the engineering colleges of the country offered naval oriented curriculum, therefore, an exclusive institute named Officers Training Section (OTS) was established at PNS KARSAZ in 1962. It was gradually equipped with suitable training aids and re-designated as Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) in May 1966 and affiliated to University of Karachi for the award of BE (Mechanical) and BE (Electrical) degrees to PN graduates. In March 1977, the college was then affiliated with NED University of Engineering & Technology Karachi. The facilities at KARSAZ became scarce with the introduction of GL Scheme of training and ever increasing number of foreign trainees, therefore, a project of comprehensive, purpose-built and independent PN Engineering College was launched and in this regard, construction of various buildings/blocks of PNEC at current location was commenced in 1980. Subsequently, a fully equipped PN Engineering College was commissioned as PNS JAUHAR on 7 September 1982 by the then Chief of the Naval Staff, Admiral Karamat Rahman Niazi. Captain (later Commodore) Qaiser Mahmud was appointed as its first Commanding Officer. Postgraduate courses were introduced in 1988 and admission facility was also opened to civilian candidates as well. Some officers of EME branch of Pakistan Army also underwent degree courses at PNEC. In 1995, it became the constituent college of National University of Sciences & Technology (NUST) and in 1998, PhD programme also commenced at PNEC. The College acquired ISO-9001 certification in June 1999 and became the first educational institution in Pakistan with such certification. Over the years, the institution has made tremendous progress and is now considered as one of the leading engineering institutions of NUST as well as the country. The College has also the pride to train a large number of naval officers from Bahrain, Qatar, Palestine, Libya, Saudi Arabia and several other Muslim countries. text: MILESTONES ACHIEVED - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) About Us MILESTONES ACHIEVED MILESTONES ACHIEVED Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) started its journey as an institute named Officers Training Section (OTS) established at PNS KARSAZ in 1962. It gradually upgraded its status and re-designated as Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) in May 1967. PNEC affiliated to University of Karachi for the award of BE (Mechanical) and BE (Electrical) degrees to PN graduates. In March 1977, the college was affiliated with NED University of Engineering & Technology Karachi. The construction of various buildings/blocks of PNEC at current location was commenced in 1980. Subsequently, a fully equipped PNEC was commissioned as PNS JAUHAR on 7 September 1982 and postgraduate courses were introduced in 1988. The admission facility was also opened to civilian candidates in 1988 and officers of EME branch of Pakistan Army also underwent degree courses. In 1995, it became the constituent college of National University of Sciences & Technology (NUST). In 1998, PhD programme also commenced and the college acquired ISO-9001 certification in June 1999 to become first educational institution in Pakistan with such certification. Milestones achieved by PNEC at a glance are: Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) Milestones Achieved Milestone Year Established as Officers Training Section (OTS) 1962 Renamed Pakistan Navy Engineering College 1966 Affiliated with the University of Karachi 1966 Affiliated with NED University of Engineering & Technology 1977 Introduction of General Line (GL) scheme of training 1980 Introduction of General Line (GL) scheme of training 1982 Construction of various blocks of PNEC at present location 1982 Commencement of MS (EE) and MS (ME) Degree Programmes 1988 Facility of admission extended to civilians students 1988 Started MS programme 1989 Construction of Gunroom Mess 1993 Became constituent college of National University of Sciences and Technology (NUST) 1995 Started PhD programme 1998 ISO 9001 certification as per 1994 standard 1999 Recertification to new quality standards i.e. ISO 9001 : 2000 2002 1st PhD qualified scholar 2005 Department of Industrial & Manufacturing Engineering established 2008 BS (MIS) Degree Programme Commenced 2009 OBE (Outcome-based Education) based Accredition for EPE (Electronics & Power Engineering) 2014 Construction of Boys Hostel 2016 Commencement of Chinese Language Course 2016 Department of Naval Architecture established 2018 Up-gradation of Professional Development Centre (PDC) 2018 BS (Maritime Sciences) Degree programme for PN Operations branch officers commenced 2019 Department of Cyber Security established 2019 Establishment of Chinese Language Lab 2019 Construction

text: COMMANDANTS HISTORY - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) About Us COMMANDANTS HISTORY COMMANDANTS HISTORY At the time of independence, Pakistan Navy did not have any suitable institution for the training of its technical officers. Since none of the engineering colleges of the country offered naval oriented curriculum, therefore, an exclusive institute named Officers Training Section (OTS) was established at PNS KARSAZ in 1962. It was gradually equipped with suitable training aids and re-designated as Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) in May 1967 and affiliated to University of Karachi for the award of BE (Mechanical) and BE (Electrical) degrees to PN graduates. In March 1977, the college was then affiliated with NED University of Engineering & Technology Karachi. The facilities at KARSAZ became scarce with the introduction of GL Scheme of training and ever increasing number of foreign trainees. Therefore, a project of comprehensive, purpose-built and independent PN Engineering College was launched and in this regard, construction of various buildings/blocks of PNEC at current location was commenced in 1980. Subsequently, a fully equipped PN Engineering College was commissioned as PNS JAUHAR on 7 September 1982 by the then Chief of the Naval Staff, Admiral Karamat Rahman Niazi SI(M) SJ. Captain (later Commodore) Qaiser Mahmud was appointed as its first Commanding Officer. Postgraduate courses were introduced in 1988 and admission facility was also opened to civilian candidates as well. Some officers of EME branch of Pakistan Army also underwent degree courses at PNEC. In 1995, it became the constituent college of National University of Sciences & Technology (NUST) and in 1998, PhD programme also commenced at PNEC. The College acquired ISO-9001 certification in June 1999 and became the first educational institution in Pakistan with such certification. Over the years, the institution has made tremendous progress and is now considered as one of the leading engineering institutions of NUST as well as the country. The College has also the pride to train a large number of naval officers from Bahrain, Qatar, Palestine, Libya, Saudi Arabia and several other Muslim countries. Total 9 commanding officers and 20 commandants have led the PNEC to date. text: Spreading Smiles, Bridging Hearts: SEEK Lab & ECSS at Markaz-e-Umeed - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News Spreading Smiles, Bridging Hearts: SEEK Lab & ECSS at Markaz-e-Umeed Spreading Smiles, Bridging Hearts: SEEK Lab & ECSS at Markaz-e-Umeed March 13, 2025 - March 13, 2025 On 13th March 2025, Volunteers from Sustainability Energy Efficiency and Knowledge (SEEK) Lab, in collaboration with Education & Community Service Society, embarked on a heartwarming journey to Markaz-e-Umeed, a school for exceptional children. Team shared gifts to children and learn sign language. A special session with Zeeshan Siddiqui (Managing Director, Markaz-e-Umeed) helped team understand the challenges faced by special children in our society and the role we can play in making a difference. In addition to gifts, the faculty and students also contributed a monetary donation to support the school and its students.
title: Spreading Smiles, Bridging Hearts: SEEK Lab & ECSS at Markaz-e-Umeed - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

The visit provided hands-on exposure to industrial manufacturing processes, including assembly lines, quality control, and automation. Students gained valuable insights into industrial workflows, with a focus on innovation and efficiency, enhancing their preparedness for future careers.

text: NUST-PNEC 36th Convocation Ceremony - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News NUST-PNEC 36th Convocation Ceremony NUST-PNEC 36th Convocation Ceremony December 27, 2024 - December 27, 2024 36th Convocation of Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) was held on 20 Dec 24 at Pakistan Navy Engineering College. Admiral Naveed Ashraf NI(M), T Bt, Chief of the Naval Staff was the Chief Guest for the occasion. Vice Admiral Muhammad Faisal Abbasi HI(M), Commander Karachi and Lt General (Retd) Dr Muhammad Zahid Latif Mirza HI(M), Rector NUST also attended the convocation. Rector NUST conferred the degrees to graduating students. A total of 372 x graduates were awarded Post and Undergraduate degrees. The Chief Guest also awarded medals to outstanding position holders. During address, the Chief Guest highlighted that PNEC under the leadership of NUST has consistently demonstrated a commitment to promote a robust culture of research and development. The Chief Guest commended PNEC initiative in launching cutting-edge discipline of Artificial Intelligence (Al), Data Science and Computer Science at the postgraduate and undergraduate level. He also congratulated Rector NUST and Commandant PNEC on the historic occasion of the graduation of first batch of BE (Naval Architecture). In the end, he extended his heartiest felicitations to the graduating students and their parents upon successful culmination of their academic endeavors. He advised the graduates to be person of strong character, sacrifice their gains for the national interest and always seek guidance and strength from ALLAH (SWT).
title: NUST-PNEC 36th Convocation Ceremony - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: Lean Manufacturing and Automation - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News Lean Manufacturing and Automation Lean Manufacturing and Automation November 12, 2024 - November 12, 2024 On 8th November 2024, Mr. Faisal Atique, Vice President at Lucky Motor Corporation, delivered an inspiring guest lecture on Lean Manufacturing and Automation at the College Auditorium. Engaging a diverse audience of Mechanical, Electrical, and Naval Architecture engineering students, Mr. Atique explored the principles of Lean Manufacturing and the transformative power of innovation in the automotive industry. His insights highlighted the integration of automation with lean practices, underscoring the potential of these strategies to drive efficiency and sustainability within the sector.
title: Lean Manufacturing and Automation - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: Department of Engineering Sciences - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) Departments Department of Engineering Sciences Department of Engineering Sciences The department of engineering sciences has its prime objective of producing highly skilled mechanical engineers with specialisation in three areas namely, Thermal Fluids, Thermal Powers and Computational Statistics. The department is equipped with modern laboratories, research facilities and state of art computer centers. Focused emphasis on practical exposure is also given to mechanical engineers along with theoretical instructional, with the help of qualified faculty and several well equipped laboratories. The Faculty of Engineering Sciences is headed by Dean Engineering sciences (Dean ES). He is assisted by three Course Coordinators to look after academics aspects of undergraduate program, post graduate program and Marine Engineering Systems Management Course (MESMC), a specialization for mechanical branch service officers. Moreover, three Group Officers look after various support facilities i.e. Laboratories, CADAM facility and workshops. The department has excellent commitments for teaching and producing graduate Engineers; it has over the years acquired national/international recognition. At present this Engineering Sciences department, besides various professional courses, offers undergraduate and postgraduate programs at Master s and PhD level only once in a year. BE Mechanical Program is accredited up to Intake Batch 2019,2020 &2021 under Level-II as per decision of PEC 110th EAB meeting held on August 02, 2023 PEC/EAD/PNEC-NUST-K/MECH/DL (110th)23. PEOs: Following are the Program Educational Objectives (PEOs) of B.E Mechanical Engineering Program: Few years after graduation, the students are expected to demonstrate their ability to: PEO-1 Apply mechanical engineering knowledge to identify and address the technical and societal problems PEO-2 Enhance their intellectual and analytical abilities in taking initiatives and/or develop innovative ideas for technological and professional growth in mechanical engineering and allied disciplines PEO-3 Work effectively as a team member or lead multidisciplinary teams while demonstrating the interpersonal & management skills and ethical responsibilities PLOs: Following are the Program Learning Outcomes (PLOs) PLO 01 Engineering Knowledge PLO 02 Problem Analysis PLO 03 Design/development of solution PLO 04 Investigation PLO 05 Tool usage PLO 06 The Engineer & society PLO 07 Ethics PLO 08 Individual and Collaborative Team work PLO 09 Individual and Team work PLO 10 Project Management and Finance PLO 11 Project Management PLO 12 Life-long learning Head of Department Message Dr Murtaza Hussain It is my pleasure and honour to welcome you to the Department of Engineering Sciences at Pakistan Navy Engineering College. Mechanical Engineering
title: Department of Engineering Sciences - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: 02 Problem Analysis PLO 03 Design/development of solution PLO 04 Investigation PLO 05 Tool usage PLO 06 The Engineer & society PLO 07 Ethics PLO 08 Individual and Collaborative Team work PLO 09 Individual and Team work PLO 10 Project Management and Finance PLO 11 Project Management PLO 12 Life-long learning Head of Department Message Dr Murtaza Hussain It is my pleasure and honour to welcome you to the Department of Engineering Sciences at Pakistan Navy Engineering College. Mechanical Engineering has been thought of traditionally as a professional discipline, applying principles from engineering, physics in design, analysis, manufacturing of mechanical systems, and the associate transfer and flow of energies from one form to another. Now, it is at the center of almost all technical advancements, from health services to communications, transportations, and all the infrastructures that you see around. The Mechanical Engineers of today are multidisciplinary, with knowledge from other branches of engineering. Department of Mechanical Engineering strives to increase the knowledge, enhance critical thinking, ability to transform information into knowledge and power of analyzing the things technically. We in the Engineering Sciences department are striving to impart knowledge through a closed knit family of highly competent faculty. Our Laboratories have been very well established and equipped to instill motivation in the students to learn beyond the defined boundaries of syllabi thereby enabling students to develop skills amongst the students to become promising engineers in the future. Students in Engineering Sciences are regularly afforded opportunities to participate in extracurricular activities so that they can polish their managerial and teamwork skills. The objective of these activities is to provide an environment for the practical application of classroom teaching. I welcome your interest in our department and wish you a very successful and enjoyable experience with us. text: Department of Electronics & Power Engineering - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) Departments Department of Electronics & Power Engineering Department of Electronics & Power Engineering The Electrical Engineering (EE) curriculum encompasses a broad background in the analysis and synthesis of electrical and electronic circuits and devices, in the mathematical tools necessary for signal processing, and in the design and analysis of modern electrical systems. At PNEC, students obtain an extensive insight into electrical engineering through courses and laboratory experience that include understanding contemporary issues that have global and societal impact. In addition, Electrical Engineering students have the opportunity to participate in major engineering design experiences that are offered through formalized courses, engineering extracurricular activities and research with faculty. BE Electrical Program is PEC (Pakistan Engineering council) accredited at OBE Level-II from Intake Batch 2014 up to 2020 The department has the following Program Educational Objectives: Few (Two to Three) years after graduation, the students are expected to demonstrate their ability: PEO-1 To demonstrate knowledge of Electrical Engineering and allied disciplines to analyse, i nvestigate, design, and develop viable solutions to contemporary engineering problems in a multidisciplinary work environment PEO-2 To collaborate and contribute in socio-economic project development and exploit domain-based knowledge of using modern hardware, software, and statistical tools to evaluate and devise sustainable solutions to emerging engineering problems, with due regard to environmental and societal concerns. PEO-3 To demonstrate professional qualities, work ethics, continuous enhancement of knowledge & skills, good verbal & writing skills, and ability to excel individually and collectively in their professional career. The graduating attributes (GAs), as defined by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC), have also been adopted by the department in its B.E Electrical Program as the Program Learning Outcomes (PLOs). These are: No. PLO Statement 1. Engineering Knowledge Apply knowledge of mathematics, natural science, engineering fundamentals, and engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. 2. Problem Analysis Identify, formulate, conduct research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences. 3. Design/Development of Solutions An ability to design solutions for complex engineering problems and design systems, components or processes that meet specified needs with appropriate considerations for public health and safety, cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. 4. Investigation Conduct investigation of complex engineering problems using research-based knowledge and research methods, including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of information

Head of Department Message Engr Ashar Mahmood The Electrical Engineering (EE) Department administers the Academic Programs for BE in the disciplines of Electronics, Electrical Power and Telecommunication), MS (Electrical Engineering) with specialization in Control Engineering and Communication Engineering, PhD (Electrical) in specialized fields and mid-career professional courses of WESMC to Weapon Engineering officers. The Department also manages college networking & internet services facility and activities/courses related to computer sciences and information technologies.

Bechelor of Engineering in Electrical Power, Electronics and Telecommunication The BE Electrical Engineering Program is designed to meet the modern trends in the field of electrical technology, encompassing wider technological perspective. The program includes study of engineering core & interdisciplinary, basic sciences & humanities, computer skills, management with blend of design project completion and presentation for useful application. The Program has been designed as per standards and guidelines provided by HEC/PEC. The unification of syllabus in terms of specialization in electronics, power, telecommunication has been introduced to meet the technological challenges, to provide trained manpower for technical solution and to incorporate market trends. The strong element of electrical power enables the graduate engineer to take on his job effectively in power generation and distribution sector. With a significant minor and major design project work, students are able to apply their theoretical knowledge into research and development activities. The electrical engineering labs are equipped with state of art devices and equipment. The electronic engineering specialization is built on strong fundamentals enabling graduates to meet the challenges of contemporary engineering fields of electronics, with engineering foundation. The curriculum lays expertise towards small-to-large scale integrated embedded systems and strong stress on laboratory-work, assignments and final outcome. The telecommunication engineering covers the provision of expertise in wireless communication and advanced technology in terms of security. By team work of the students, faculty members and individuals from the enriched local industry at Karachi and above all the special help of Allah the Almighty we will be able to achieve our Vision & Mission. Lets join hands to work together and make it a success. text: Department of Naval Architecture - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) Departments Department of Naval Architecture Department of Naval Architecture Department of Naval Architecture is a recently established department and was formed in the year 2018. Both Postgraduate & Undergraduate programs in the field of Naval Architecture are being offered. A naval architect is a professional engineer who is responsible for the design, classification, survey, construction, and repair of both commercial and military ships including yachts, powerboats, fishing boats, barges, warships, cruise ships, ice breakers, submersibles, submarines and offshore structures. The course curriculum has been designed to provide knowledge in all core areas of field of Naval Architecture such as Marine Hydrodynamics, Marine Structure and Advanced Marine Dynamics. Students also undertake a comprehensive ship design exercise as a group project to learn to carryout concept studies in ship designing. During design exercise, special emphasis is given on inculcating interpersonal and team skills so that they may be easily integrated into industrial ship design teams. PEO-1 Apply Naval Architecture knowledge to identify and address the technical and societal problems PEO-2 Enhance their intellectual and analytical abilities in taking initiatives and/or develop innovative ideas for technological and professional growth in the field of Naval Architecture and allied disciplines PEO-3 Work effectively as a team member or lead multidisciplinary teams while demonstrating the interpersonal & management skills and ethical responsibilities No. PLO Statement 1. Engineering Knowledge An ability to apply knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems 2. Problem Analysis An ability to identify, formulate, research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences and engineering sciences. 3. Design/Development of solutions An ability to design solutions for complex engineering problems and design systems, components or processes that meet specified needs with appropriate consideration for public health and safety, cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. 4. Investigation An ability to investigate complex engineering problems in a methodical way including literature survey, design and conduct of experiments, analysis and interpretation of experimental data, and synthesis of information to derive valid conclusions. 5. Modern Tool usage An ability to create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools, including prediction and modeling, to complex engineering activities, with an understanding of the limitations. 6. The Engineer & society An ability to apply reasoning informed by contextual knowledge

Head of Department Message Dr Faheem Ur Rehman Established in September 2018, the department setoff its journey by launching MS in Naval Architecture, followed by the undergraduate program in Fall 20. We are dedicated to shaping future leaders in Naval Architecture through a rigorous academic environment grounded in innovation, intellectual growth, and societal impact. Our mission centers on technical excellence, creativity, and ethical leadership. We equip our students with the skills to address complex challenges, generate innovative solutions, and advance their careers in Naval Architecture and related fields. Graduates are prepared to meet industry demands, demonstrating their competencies while considering global and societal impacts. Teamwork and ethical responsibility are integral to our curriculum, enabling students to thrive in multidisciplinary teams and take on leadership roles.

text: Department of Applied Sciences - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) Departments Department of Applied Sciences Department of Applied Sciences Welcome to the Faculty of Applied Sciences at Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC). We are committed to teaching and research excellence in Applied Sciences. We have a faculty well equipped with pedagogy and teaching learning techniques. The department undertakes intellectual grooming of BE and MS students in pure sciences (Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics) and humanities (English, Pakistan Study and Islamiat). The department is aligned with PNEC s academic Mission to provide high-quality, student-centered learning experiences. We have 18 faculty members and more than 19 staff for exploring opportunities arising from needs of other departments. We keep on reviewing our curricula on regular basis to remain in step with educational policy of Pakistan. We enhance the knowledge through scaffolding, changing schemata and work on cognitive domain. Head of Department Message Mr Adnan Safdar It gives me immense pleasure to welcome you to the Faculty of Applied Sciences at Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC). The Department of Applied Sciences continues to build on a tradition of excellence and innovation in research, teaching and services to create a vibrant community of faculty which ensures that undergraduate students are ready to make a difference at national and international levels. We are committed to hone leadership and communication skills in our students which transform them into the most sought - after individuals, equipped with professional skills to pursue their careers. Keeping ourselves aligned with PNEC's Academic Mission to provide high quality, student-centered learning experiences, the quality and drive of our faculty and their commitment to excellence enables students to develop focus in selected areas of studies by integrating a rich understanding of communication theory and research. We ensure the development of marketable skills and abilities in our students to take leadership positions in their communities. Being a technology-focused faculty, we never underestimate the importance of keeping abreast of changing industry trends. Therefore, reviewing of curricula in line with the valuable input of our External Advisory Council of industry partners is a regular feature at our department. Owing to the increasing importance of Chinese Language Learning in the wake of CPEC, we have introduced Chinese Language Course for undergraduate students as an optional subject. Furthermore, Arabic Language Course has also been introduced so as to inculcate religious values in the students through a better understanding of Qur'an.

text: Department of Industrial and Manufacturing Engineering - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) Departments Department of Industrial and Manufacturing Engineering Department of Industrial and Manufacturing Engineering The department offers post-graduate degree courses in manufacturing engineering and management. The program is designed to cater for the challenging requirement of the manufacturing industry while offering courses in technical management to equip the graduates with both technical and management skills. Head of Department Message Dr Faheem Ur Rehman Department of Industrial & Manufacturing Engineering was established in 2007 to offer post-graduate programmes in Manufacturing Engineering and Management. Since then, the department is running full time MS programme in the evening. In addition, the Ph.D. programme is also running full time. The training imparted to students in all the programmes, involves theoretical knowledge and laboratory work in the various subjects of Industrial Engineering, Manufacturing Engineering, Engineering Management and Interdisciplinary subjects relevant to the programme. Students of Industrial and Manufacturing Department have been working with local industry on various projects since the start of the programs. Industry projects help the students to polish their concepts and gain confidence by putting theoretical knowledge into practice. All the projects are supervised by the faculty as well as by experienced professionals in the industry. Alhamdolillah, over the years, the department has successfully built its reputation in the local industry and the graduates are well accepted. The major subject area of the programmes are: 1- Manufacturing Processes, Systems, and Technologies 2- Quality Management 3- Product Development 4- Production Management and Economics 5- Supply Chain Management 6- Operations Research 7- Industrial Health and Safety 8- Project Management 9- Entrepreneurship 10- Robotics and Automation Read More Departments Department of Engineering Sciences Department Faculty Department of Electronics & Power Engineering Department Faculty Department of Cyber Security Department Faculty Department of Naval Architecture Department Faculty Department of Maritime Sciences Department Faculty Department of Applied Sciences Department Faculty Department of Industrial and Manufacturing Engineering Department Faculty Department of Computer Science Department Faculty VISION & MISSION VISION This degree programme is designed to produce Industrial & Manufacturing Engineers who can meet real time challenges of the industry by demonstrating their competencies in learned methodologies and skills acquired during the training by virtue of theoretical and practical knowledge gained during undergraduate and postgraduate programme. text: Department of Computer Science - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) Departments Department of Computer Science Department of Computer Science Computer Science Department has been established at NUST- PNEC in June 2025. The primary objective of the department in to effective run and manage the BSCS and MS Data Science Programs which has been launched w.e.f Fall 2025 after obtaining NOC from NCEAC & HEC in July 2025. Our aim is to make it centre of Academic Excellence in field of Computing Research in Pakistan and the World. Head of Department Message Uzma Khalid Welcome to the Department of Computer Science at PNEC-NUST. Our vision is to be a global leader in computer science education and research and fostering innovations that make a significant positive impact on society. The department s commitment to excellence is reflected in our comprehensive curriculum. We offer a variety of programs including BS (Computer Science) MS (Cyber Security) and MS (Data Science). MS Cyber Security at NUST-PNEC is the first program with this title in Pakistan. Our programs provide a solid foundation in the theoretical aspects of computer science, advanced programming techniques, and specialized expertise in contemporary computer applications. Faculty of Computer Science is committed to provide an attractive opportunity for students with dedicated and competent faculty and vibrant academic atmosphere. It is the only most beautiful constituent college of NUST in Southern Pakistan. We ensure that our graduates are not only ready to excel in the job market but also are well-prepared to pursue advanced studies and entrepreneurial ventures. Our faculty members are recognized for their high-quality publications, international collaborations, and robust industry partnerships. Their dedication to impactful research addresses real-world challenges and keeps our students up-to-date with the development and usage of cutting edge technologies. By team work of the students, faculty members and individuals from the enriched local industry at Karachi and above all the special help of Allah the Almighty we will be able to achieve our Vision & Mission. Lets join hands to work together and make it a success. Mission To provide quality education and advanced training in computer science domain through well-structured undergraduate and postgraduate programs. The department is committed to stimulating intellectual curiosity, critical thinking, and lifelong learning while promoting research, innovation, and ethical practices in computing. It aims to nurture competent graduates, researchers, and technology leaders capable of addressing contemporary and future technological challenges. Through strong industry linkages, interdisciplinary collaboration, and adherence to the core values of NUST and PNEC, department strives to contribute to sustainable development and positively impact society at the national and global levels. text: About Us - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) About Us About PNEC Pakistan Navy Engineering College was initially affiliated with Karachi University. In 1977 the affiliation of PNEC was transferred to NED University of Engineering & Technology. The college was shifted to its present location in 1982 and commissioned as PNS JAUHAR. In 1995 it became the constituent College of National University Of Sciences and Technology (NUST) Islamabad. The college conducts undergraduate courses in disciplines of Electrical, Mechanical and Naval Architecture. The College also conducts MS and PhD programs in Mechanical, Electrical and Manufacturing Engineering for service and civilian students. PNEC also stands out by launching MS in the specialised field of Naval Architecture and Cyber Security from Fall 18 to Spring 20 respectively. COMMANDANT MESSAGE Engr Mudassar Khurshid PNEC from its initial beginnings as an engineering school that was established back in 1962 and affiliated with NED University Karachi, has transformed and transitioned into a large constituent campus of National University of Sciences and Technology (NUST); serving as an iconic centre of excellence in engineering education. Extending the frontiers of education, PNEC started MS programme in 80s and PhD programme in 2000. It was the first NUST constituent college from where PhD scholar graduated under NUST. Moving with the time, PNEC continues to offer and add more disciplines to its fold. What distinguishes PNEC from others is our research throughout which is more focused towards application, providing solutions to technological challenges facing the organizations. Research projects from Pakistan Navy, ICT R&D, HEC, Maritime Security Agency, to name a few. I stand convinced that has all been realizable because of our dedicated and qualified faculty who have been inspiring and mentoring students. Our graduates and their successful careers are further evidences to the quality of training and mentoring they have received being a student at this university. With global footprint, our alumni extends to the stretches of the world. text: OPEN HOUSE NUST-PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News OPEN HOUSE NUST-PNEC OPEN HOUSE NUST-PNEC May 17, 2023 - May 17, 2023 PNEC successfully conducted Open-House and on-spot interviews on May 17, 2023. There were 32 projects on display, covering disciplines of Electrical and Mechanical Engineering. Around 78 x Senior officials of HR/ technical representatives from 44 x renowned local industries visited PNEC to witness the projects. Moreover, NUST Formula Student Team (NFST) and Team Shell-Eco Marathon displayed their vehicles at the venue. General overview of Naval Architecture Department and courses being offered at Professional Development Centre (PDC) was also part of the event. In addition, 06 x companies conducted on-spot interviews at the Open-House, providing students with an opportunity to apply for jobs directly with industry. Technical acumen and professional conduct of the students was appreciated by the interviewers.
title: OPEN HOUSE NUST-PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: DR SAMEENA ZEHRA, ECONOMIC MINISTER AT PAKISTANI EMBASSY IN UK VISITED NUST-PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News DR SAMEENA ZEHRA, ECONOMIC MINISTER AT PAKISTANI EMBASSY IN UK VISITED NUST-PNEC DR SAMEENA ZEHRA, ECONOMIC MINISTER AT PAKISTANI EMBASSY IN UK VISITED NUST-PNEC December 05, 2023 - December 05, 2023 05-Dec-23 marked a significant moment as Dr. Sameena Zehra, Economic Minister at Pakistani embassy in UK, paid a visit to NUST-PNEC to engage in a comprehensive discussion about the remarkable progress and achievements of the NUST Formula Student Team (NFST). As we delved into conversation, the focal point became the venues of collaboration and advancement of STEM education within the Pakistani educational system. Dr. Zehra, Dr. Sajjad Haider Zaidi Director Q.A and Dr Mohsin Director PDC graced the meeting with their presence, emphasized the pivotal role played by NFST in fostering this advancement. The synergy between industry and academia, coupled with the F1 in School initiative, emerged as a driving force behind this progress. The discussion extended to a fruitful meeting with the Commandant PNEC, where the Economic Minister explored avenues for further collaboration to promote Formula Student culture in Pakistan. Few glimpses of today are following;

text: NUST Formula Student Team (NFST) of NUST-PNEC and SOAR STEM - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News NUST Formula Student Team (NFST) of NUST-PNEC and SOAR STEM NUST Formula Student Team (NFST) of NUST-PNEC and SOAR STEM September 26, 2023 - September 26, 2023 In a promising development for education and innovation in Pakistan, NUST Formula Student Team (NFST) of NUST-PNEC and SOAR STEM have officially joined hands on 26-September-2023.A significant collaboration aimed at bolstering STEM education across the nation. Specifically, the partnership intends to initiate F1 in Schools, a global STEM challenge, in Pakistan. Mr. Osman Rashid, Founder and CEO of SOAR STEM, made a notable visit to NUST-PNEC, where he engaged in a productive discussion about the roadmap for introducing F1 in Schools in Pakistan and how it can immensely benefit the educational landscape. He met with Engr. Tauqeer Ahmed Khawaja Commandant NUST-PNEC, Dr Syed Sajjad Haider Zaidi, DQA, Dr. Mohsin Ali, DPDC, Mr. Ziauddin Siddiqui, Advisor of NFST and Mr. M Umer, Team Lead, to explore this initiative. This insightful meetup was followed by a momentous signing ceremony, solidifying the partnership between SOAR and NUST NFST. The unified goal of this collaboration is to propel Pakistan towards becoming a prominent hub for talent and innovation, with a particular focus on nurturing young minds through the exciting F1 in Schools program.
title: NUST Formula Student Team (NFST) of NUST-PNEC and SOAR STEM - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: Students from Semester III ME (B1 & B2) Visited the Pakistan Machine Tool Factory - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News Students from Semester III ME (B1 & B2) Visited the Pakistan Machine Tool Factory Students from Semester III ME (B1 & B2) Visited the Pakistan Machine Tool Factory October 21, 2024 - October 21, 2024 On 21 October 2024, Students from Semester III ME (B1 & B2) visited the Pakistan Machine Tool Factory along with Faculty Members Dr. Adil Loya and Mr. Ahsan Khan. PMTF stands out as a leading manufacturer specializing in both conventional and CNC machine tools, along with automotive parts and pressure die-cast components. text: Students from Semester V-B2 EE Visited Lotte Chemical Pakistan Limited (LCPL) - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News Students from Semester V-B2 EE Visited Lotte Chemical Pakistan Limited (LCPL) Students from Semester V-B2 EE Visited Lotte Chemical Pakistan Limited (LCPL) October 24, 2024 - October 24, 2024 On 24 October 2024, Students from Semester V-B2 EE visited Lotte Chemical Pakistan Limited (LCPL) along with Faculty Member Mr. Sajid Mehmood. text: SPEAK OUT FOR ENGINEERING (SOFE) NATIONALS 2023 PRESENTATION COMPETITION - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News SPEAK OUT FOR ENGINEERING (SOFE) NATIONALS 2023 PRESENTATION COMPETITION SPEAK OUT FOR ENGINEERING (SOFE) NATIONALS 2023 PRESENTATION COMPETITION December 06, 2023 - December 06, 2023 IMechE NUST-PNEC Student Chapter organized Speak Out for Engineering (SOfE) Nationals 2023 presentation competition on 06 Dec 23. Aspiring engineers from prestigious institutions such as NUST-PNEC, MUET Jamshoro, NEDUET, and UET Taxila participated in the competition. Distinguished presence of Dr. Muhammad Tufail, Chair IMechE Pakistan Group (Pro-VC NED University), as the Chief Guest, added significant weight to the occasion. The judging panel, composed of accomplished professionals lent their expertise to evaluate the participants presentations. Each presentation reflected not only technical prowess but also a remarkable ability to articulate complex engineering concepts with clarity and confidence. Benazir Memon from MUET Jamshoro was declared the winner, showcasing exceptional communication skills and engineering acumen. Shahmir Khan, from NUST-PNEC, secured the Runners-up position, his presentation lauded for its ingenuity and clarity, merely four points behind the winner. The Speak Out for Engineering Presentation Competition remains a beacon for aspiring engineers, providing a platform to showcase their talents, fostering effective communication, and steering the future of engineering towards clarity, innovation, and proficiency in expression.
title: SPEAK OUT FOR ENGINEERING (SOFE) NATIONALS 2023 PRESENTATION COMPETITION - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: Pakistan Sustainability Week 2023 - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News Pakistan Sustainability Week 2023 Pakistan Sustainability Week 2023 September 07, 2023 - September 09, 2023 The Pakistan Sustainability Week 2023, held at the Expo Centre Karachi from September 7th to 9th 2023, stands as a resounding success, serving as a dynamic platform that united academics, industry professionals, students, and passionate environmental enthusiasts to confront and deliberate upon the most pressing sustainability issues of our time. The distinguished guest of honor, Director Q.A NUST-PNEC Dr Syed Sajjad Haider Zaidi, graced the event s inauguration, bestowing upon it a sense of prestige and significance. The event was enriched by insightful panel discussions led by luminaries such as Dr. Arshad Aziz and Dr. Tahir Abdul Hussain Ratlamwala from NUST-PNEC, offering valuable perspectives on sustainable practices. Students, true to their commitment to environmental stewardship, showcased a myriad of innovative projects aimed at addressing pressing environmental challenges. Furthermore, the event served as a fertile ground for fostering research collaborations, led by Dr. Imtiaz Hussain, the focal person, and guided by the vision of Dr. Bilal M. Khan, Director of Research. Mr. Naeem Raza, Industry Liaison Officer at NUST-PNEC, played a pivotal role in facilitating industry-academia partnerships, reinforcing the importance of collaborative efforts in advancing sustainability. The presence of leading solar panel manufacturers and distributors underscored the paramount role of renewable energy in the sustainability landscape, offering attendees an invaluable glimpse into cutting-edge solar technology. A significant highlight of the event was the remarkable achievement of the NUST PNEC Team Envision, clinching the first position in the Projects Competition at Pakistan Sustainability Week (PSW) 2023 at the Karachi Expo Centre. This triumph serves as a testament to our unwavering dedication to sustainability and innovation, showcasing our commitment to spearheading solutions for the most pressing environmental challenges.
title: Pakistan Sustainability Week 2023 - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: NUST-PNEC Formula Student Team's Achievement at Formula Student UK 2023 - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News NUST-PNEC Formula Student Team s Achievement at Formula Student UK 2023 NUST-PNEC Formula Student Team s Achievement at Formula Student UK 2023 July 19, 2023 - July 23, 2023 Formula Student Team of NUST-PNEC participated in the prestigious Formula Student UK 2023, Concept Class which was held on 19 to 23-July-2023 at United Kingdom. Remarkably, NFST achieved a historic feat for Pakistan by becoming the first and only team from the country to secure a category victory in the LTS (Lap Time Simulation) Event. The team showcased their innovative and well-designed vehicle, earning them a commendable 6th position in the Design Event, while also excelling in the Cost and Manufacturing Event, securing another 6th position. NUST-PNEC Formula Student Team clinched an outstanding overall 5th position in the Concept Class category, a remarkable achievement that solidifies their standing as one of the top teams in the international Formula Student circuit. Some pictorial glimpses are as under:
title: NUST-PNEC Formula Student Team's Achievement at Formula Student UK 2023 - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: NRT (NUST ROCKET TEAM) - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News NRT (NUST ROCKET TEAM) NRT (NUST ROCKET TEAM) October 01, 2022 - October 01, 2022 In April 2022, NUST Rocket Team signed a liaison agreement with Pakistan s prestigious space agency, SUPARCO. text: PROCTER & GAMBLE VISITED NUST-PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News PROCTER & GAMBLE VISITED NUST-PNEC PROCTER & GAMBLE VISITED NUST-PNEC February 26, 2024 - February 26, 2024 On 26 Feb 2024, Procter & Gamble organized a Recruitment Drive at the NUST-PNEC. A meeting was held with officials of Procter & Gamble to discuss job opportunities, internships, and potential research collaborations, emphasizing the importance of bridging academia-industry gaps. Massive turnout of students showcased exceptional talent and enthusiasm. In recognition of the successful collaboration, Commandant PNEC Engr Tauqeer Ahmad Khawaja along with Dr. Syed Sajjad Haider Zaidi (Director QA) awarded the Crest to Head of Procter & Gamble team and expressed appreciation to their team members.
title: PROCTER & GAMBLE VISITED NUST-PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: NATIONAL FOOD VISITED NUST-PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News NATIONAL FOOD VISITED NUST-PNEC NATIONAL FOOD VISITED NUST-PNEC February 13, 2024 - February 13, 2024 On 13 Feb 2024, National Food Limited hosted a highly successful Campus Recruitment Drive at the National University of Sciences and Technology (NUST)-PNEC, wherein students specializing in Mechanical, Electrical, and Naval Architecture disciplines were provided with the opportunity to engage in interviews conducted by HR team. The event, marked by meticulous organization and enthusiastic participation, offered invaluable insights into potential internship and job placements within our organization. Additionally, attendees benefited from complimentary refreshments and had access to dedicated stalls providing career guidance Special thanks to Asma Bhutto, Sadia Imran, Fareezeh Sidhwa, Maha Mustafa, Ahsan Ateeq, Syed Riaz Khalid, and Sidra Azam for their remarkable contributions in arranging and supporting this event. Acknowledgments are extended to Naeem Raza (Deputy Manager CC & IL) for their exceptional efforts in orchestrating this event.
title: NATIONAL FOOD VISITED NUST-PNEC - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)

text: Guest Lecture: Difference Between Islamic and Conventional Banking - Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC) News Guest Lecture: Difference Between Islamic and Conventional Banking Guest Lecture: Difference Between Islamic and Conventional Banking September 09, 2024 - September 09, 2024 A guest lecture on the topic Difference Between Islamic and Conventional Banking was arranged by RMO Department on 4th September 2024 at college auditorium.
